---
title: "Credit Card Trx Fraud Detection"
author: "Fariza Zakaria"
date: "2025-01-08"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---

![Credit Card](G:\\My Drive\\Data analyst Roadmap\\Portfolio\\R Languange\\credit_card_image.png)


## R Markdown

```{r Set a CRAN mirror}
options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"))

```

```{r Install missing packages}
install.packages("dplyr")
library(dplyr)
```
### Setup & Loagin Packages

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
install.packages("DT")

library(tidyverse)  # For data manipulation and visualization
library(lubridate)  # For handling date/time
library(ggplot2)    # For advanced plotting
library(DT)         # For interactive tables
library(knitr)      # For knitting reports

```

### Load Dataset

```{r Load data}
# Adjust file paths as per your system

fraud_train <- read.csv("G:\\My Drive\\Data analyst Roadmap\\Portfolio\\R Languange\\Credit Card - Kaggle\\fraudTrain_V2.csv")

fraud_test <- read.csv("G:\\My Drive\\Data analyst Roadmap\\Portfolio\\R Languange\\Credit Card - Kaggle\\fraudTest_V2.csv")

setwd("G:\\My Drive\\Data analyst Roadmap\\Portfolio\\R Languange\\Credit Card - Kaggle")

list.files("G:\\My Drive\\Data analyst Roadmap\\Portfolio\\R Languange\\Credit Card - Kaggle")

# View the structure of the data
str(fraud_train)
str(fraud_test)

# Preview datasets
head(fraud_train)
head(fraud_test)
```

### Data Cleaning
#### Check for Duplicates

```{r Remove duplicates}
fraud_train <- fraud_train %>% distinct()
fraud_test <- fraud_test %>% distinct()
```

#### Check for Missing Values

```{r Handle missing values}
# Remove rows with missing values
fraud_train <- fraud_train %>% drop_na()
fraud_test <- fraud_test %>% drop_na()
```

#### Format Data Types

```{r Format data types}
#Check structures date column
head(fraud_train$trans_date)
head(fraud_test$trans_date)

#Handle different formats
fraud_train$trans_date <- mdy(fraud_train$trans_date)
fraud_test$trans_date <- mdy(fraud_test$trans_date)

# Convert `is_fraud` to a factor for classification
fraud_train$is_fraud <- as.factor(fraud_train$is_fraud)
fraud_test$is_fraud <- as.factor(fraud_test$is_fraud)

# Convert dates to date objects
fraud_train$trans_date <- ymd(fraud_train$trans_date)
fraud_test$trans_date <- ymd(fraud_test$trans_date)
```

### Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) 
#### Summary Statistics

```{r Summary statistics}
summary(fraud_train)
summary(fraud_test)
```

#### Fraud Distribution by Category & Amount

```{r Fraud by category and amount}
# Aggregate fraud instances by category
fraud_category <- fraud_train %>%
  group_by(category, is_fraud) %>%
  summarise(total = n(), avg_amt = mean(amt, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total))

datatable(fraud_category)  # Interactive table
```

#### Geospatial Analsis (Fraud by State)

```{r Geospatial analysis}
# Fraud rates across states
fraud_state <- fraud_train %>%
  group_by(state) %>%
  summarise(fraud_rate = mean(as.numeric(is_fraud)))

# Plot fraud rates across states
ggplot(fraud_state, aes(x = reorder(state, -fraud_rate), y = fraud_rate)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Fraud Rates by State", x = "State", y = "Fraud Rate")
```

#### Age & Fraud Analysis

```{r Age analysis}
# Calculate age
fraud_train <- fraud_train %>%
  mutate(age = as.numeric(difftime(ymd("2020-12-31"), ymd(dob), units = "weeks")) / 52.25)

# Fraud by age
ggplot(fraud_train, aes(x = age, fill = is_fraud)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, alpha = 0.7, position = "identity") +
  labs(title = "Fraud Distribution by Age", x = "Age", y = "Count")
```

#### Modelling (Optional)

```{r Modeling}
library(caret)

# Train-test split
set.seed(123)
trainIndex <- createDataPartition(fraud_train$is_fraud, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
trainData <- fraud_train[trainIndex, ]
testData <- fraud_train[-trainIndex, ]

# Train logistic regression model
model <- glm(is_fraud ~ amt + category + city_pop, data = trainData, family = "binomial")

# Predict on test data
predictions <- predict(model, testData, type = "response")
testData$predicted <- ifelse(predictions > 0.5, 1, 0)

# Confusion matrix
confusionMatrix(as.factor(testData$predicted), testData$is_fraud)
```
